title: Hilary Duff Talks ‘Complicated’ Family Dynamics Amid Rumored Rift & Past ‘Inappropriate’ Age-Gap Relationship
summary: The singer addresses it all on her upcoming album Luck ... or Something.
body: Hilary Duff ‘s upcoming album Luck … or Something covers a lot of ground, from the challenges of being a mom to having an “inappropriate” age-gap relationship when she was younger and dealing with tensions with members of her family. And in a Glamour cover story published Tuesday (Feb. 17), the singer/actress opened up about why she decided to bear it all on the new project. “I just felt really ready to share,” she explained. “I wanted to make something that I could connect with people again on the level of who I am now. I felt like people have definitely gone through some of the similar large strokes that I have in the past 10 to 15 years.” Related Ashley Tisdale & Hilary Duff’s Mom Group Drama Explained Ashley Tisdale & Haylie Duff Have a Playdate After Mom Group Drama Hilary Duff Announces Sprawling Lucky Me World Tour, Her First in Nearly 2 Decades: See the Dates “That’s my family,” she said of tracks such as “We Don’t Talk,” which seemingly addresses her rumored rift with sister Haylie, and “The Optimist,” on which she sings about wishing her “father would really love” her. “Those a…
